narrative: OMX30 opens with tailwinds from continental Europe's strong overnight close, particularly DAX's +1.71% surge, supported by a weighted correlation signal of +1.063%. The index remains well-positioned above the 200-MA (+8.0%) in a confirmed bull market, though technical momentum shows signs of exhaustion: Stochastic K at 81.5 signals overbought conditions, MACD has rolled below signal (histogram -2.538), and weak ADX (11.2) indicates directional conviction is fading. The spike in US 10Y yields to 4.487% creates a mixed backdrop—positive for Swedish banks' NIM expectations but a headwind for tech DCF valuations. Copper remains firm and gold adds 0.86%, supporting cyclicals and mining. Key risk: mean reversion bias given overbought technicals and ADX weakness—any weakness from 3180 resistance could spark quick profit-taking.
morningBrief: OMX30 set to open +0.4% to +0.8% on continental strength and positive overnight correlation. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken, Swedbank) are the primary beneficiary of yield expansion to 4.487%, offsetting some growth-sector pressure. Watch 3180 (50-MA) for resistance; support lies at 3085 (lower Bollinger band). Industrials (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Sandvik) can take advantage of firm commodity prices and stable USD/SEK around 9.38, though oil's -2.65% decline tempers input-cost tailwinds. Boliden deserves focus—gold +0.86% and copper +0.09% both support mining. Ericsson/Hexagon may lag on continued NASDAQ softness (+0.31%). Key watch: ADX below 18 means confidence should stay Medium; any break below the 50-MA or intraday reversal off 3180 could signal overbought washout into afternoon US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET.
drivers: Strong continental Europe momentum (DAX +1.71%, CAC +1.03%) with positive correlation signal of +1.063%
OMX30 firmly above 200-MA (+8.0%) in established bull market structure
Yield surge (US 10Y +54bp to 4.487%) supports bank NIM expansion despite tech DCF pressure
Weak ADX (11.2) and overbought Stochastic (K=81.5) suggest consolidation rather than breakout

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +54bp to 4.487% significantly widens net interest margin expectations; EUR/SEK stable at 10.89 supports deposit margins.
Continental Europe momentum strong (DAX +1.71%); USD/SEK stable and EUR/USD +0.10% support export competitiveness. Brent oil -2.65% eases input costs slightly.
Gold +0.86% and copper +0.09% both provide direct revenue uplift; Boliden directionally favored over SSAB given commodity exposure.
NASDAQ +0.31% is weak; higher US yields compress DCF multiples on growth names. Ericsson may underperform on sentiment.
Yield spike to 4.487% raises refinancing costs and caps property valuations. 200bp+ yield moves typically pressure REITs.
Oil decline -2.65% moderately supports consumer spending, but yield rise pressures discount rates. EUR/SEK flat limits H&M sourcing advantage.

watchList.reason: Gold +0.86% and copper +0.09% are direct tailwinds to mining revenue; commodity strength in a bull market supports cyclical upside.
US 10Y +54bp to 4.487% widens NIM significantly; Swedish banks are major beneficiaries of yield steepness.
Industrial exporter with strong DAX/continental EU backdrop; stable USD/SEK supports margin. Monitor for consolidation below 3180.
NASDAQ underperforming (+0.31%), and yields rising pressure growth DCF. Weak ADX suggests downside could accelerate in lower-conviction environment.
Oil down -2.65% is modestly supportive for consumer, but EUR/SEK flat and higher yields dampen retail appeal.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—key late-morning catalyst. No major Swedish releases scheduled. Avoid OMX30 large position adjustments until claims data.
topRisk: Overbought technicals (Stochastic K=81.5, MACD rolling over) set stage for mean-reversion pullback; weak ADX (11.2) confirms low directional conviction. Any break below 3123 or 3085 could spark rapid profit-taking into afternoon US data, especially if yields stabilize.
technicalSummary: Overbought warning: Stochastic K=81.5 >> D=51.0 (classic mean-reversion setup)
MACD deteriorating: below signal line with negative histogram (-2.538)
ADX critically weak at 11.2 (below 18 threshold)—reduce confidence to Medium, cut magnitude by 30%
Bollinger %B=0.72 upper half—room to upper band but overbought context limits upside
52W range 91% consumed; only -2.2% from all-time high (limited upside room)
